summary: DHH praises Manning Publications for their ebook offerings, noting he's been reading Java-focused books on his phone using Repligo. He mentions extracting ideas from 'Art of Java Web Development' for Rails development and expresses interest in upcoming Manning titles. The post ends with a subtle wish that Manning would publish books about Ruby, the language he cares about most. Written in the earliest days of Rails, it reveals DHH's cross-pollination approach to framework design.
key_points: DHH was actively mining Java web development books for ideas to incorporate into Rails
He was an early adopter of reading ebooks on mobile phones in 2004
Manning Publications was his preferred technical publisher due to ebook availability
He wished Manning would publish Ruby books, hinting at Ruby's underserved status in 2004
money_quotes: I recently finished reading Art of Java Web Development (nicking all the good ideas I could find for Rails)
If only Manning would branch out a little and start offering books on the language I care about the most.
key_insight: Rails was built by deliberately studying and extracting the best ideas from the Java web development ecosystem, then reimagining them in Ruby.
